title: euphorbia pubescens var. leucotricha
intro: Euphorbia pubescens var. leucotricha, also known as the woolly spurge or desert spurge, is a succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The woolly spurge has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin conditions, and aiding in wound healing, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpenoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
